evals.prompt: How do I set up a Go HTTP server using the standard library with route grouping?
evals.expected_output: Use chi router instead of http.DefaultServeMux. Create chi.NewRouter(), register middleware with r.Use(), group routes with r.Route('/v1', func(r chi.Router) {...}). Never use http.DefaultServeMux — always create a named router. Mount sub-routers with r.Mount().

evals.prompt: How do I write middleware using net/http that injects a value into the request context?
evals.expected_output: Write a function that takes http.Handler and returns http.Handler. Call next.ServeHTTP(w, r.WithContext(ctx)) with the enriched context. Never write to w after calling next.ServeHTTP — headers are already sent. Use r.WithContext() to create a new request with the modified context.

evals.prompt: What are the key differences between using net/http directly versus Gin or Echo?
evals.expected_output: net/http requires manual JSON decoding (json.NewDecoder), header setting (w.Header().Set), and status writing (w.WriteHeader). Always set Content-Type before writing body. Call w.WriteHeader() before w.Write() — implicit 200 is sent on first Write. Use chi for routing patterns, middleware composition, and URL parameter extraction via chi.URLParam(r, 'id').

should_not_trigger: How do I configure the Gin engine with custom recovery middleware?
Set up graceful shutdown with SIGTERM handling in a Go server
Write a mockgen directive for a Go store interface
